title: I Meant To Do My Work To-Day
content:
I meant to do my work to-day - 
        But a brown bird sang in the apple-tree,
    And a butterfly flitted across the field,
        And all the leaves were calling me.

    And the wind went sighing over the land,
        Tossing the grasses to and fro,
    And a rainbow held out its shining hand - 
        So what could I do but laugh and go?
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Richard Le Gallienne delivers a powerful performance in "I Meant To Do My Work To-Day"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
